root_text.an4.183:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.an4.183:1.2: Atha kho vassakāro brāhmaṇo magadhamahāmatto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an4.183:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vassakāro brāhmaṇo magadhamahāmatto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.183:2.3: evaṁ me diṭṭhanti, natthi tato doso;
root_text.an4.183:2.5: evaṁ me sutanti, natthi tato doso;
root_text.an4.183:2.7: evaṁ me mutanti, natthi tato doso;
root_text.an4.183:2.9: evaṁ me viññātanti, natthi tato doso’”ti.
root_text.an4.183:3.2: na panāhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sabbaṁ diṭṭhaṁ na bhāsitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an4.183:3.3: nāhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sabbaṁ sutaṁ bhāsitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an4.183:3.4: na panāhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sabbaṁ sutaṁ na bhāsitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an4.183:3.5: nāhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sabbaṁ mutaṁ bhāsitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an4.183:3.6: na panāhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sabbaṁ mutaṁ na bhāsitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an4.183:3.7: nāhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sabbaṁ viññātaṁ bhāsitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an4.183:3.8: na panāhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sabbaṁ viññātaṁ na bhāsitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.183:4.1: Yañhi, brāhmaṇa, diṭṭhaṁ bhāsato akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, evarūpaṁ diṭṭhaṁ na bhāsitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.183:4.2: Yañca khvassa, brāhmaṇa, diṭṭhaṁ abhāsato kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, evarūpaṁ diṭṭhaṁ bhāsitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.183:5.1: Yañhi, brāhmaṇa, sutaṁ bhāsato akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, evarūpaṁ sutaṁ na bhāsitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.183:5.2: Yañca khvassa, brāhmaṇa, sutaṁ abhāsato kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, evarūpaṁ sutaṁ bhāsitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.183:6.1: Yañhi, brāhmaṇa, mutaṁ bhāsato akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, evarūpaṁ mutaṁ na bhāsitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.183:6.2: Yañca khvassa, brāhmaṇa, mutaṁ abhāsato kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, evarūpaṁ mutaṁ bhāsitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.183:7.1: Yañhi, brāhmaṇa, viññātaṁ bhāsato akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, evarūpaṁ viññātaṁ na bhāsitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.183:7.2: Yañca khvassa, brāhmaṇa, viññātaṁ abhāsato kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, evarūpaṁ viññātaṁ bhāsitabbanti vadāmī”ti.
root_text.an4.183:8.1: Atha kho vassakāro brāhmaṇo magadhamahāmatto bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmīti.

translation_text.an4.183:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.an4.183:1.2: Then Vassakāra the brahmin, a chief minister of Magadha, went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an4.183:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an4.183:2.1: “Worthy Gotama, this is my doctrine and view:
translation_text.an4.183:2.2: There’s nothing wrong with talking about what you’ve seen, saying:
translation_text.an4.183:2.4: There’s nothing wrong with talking about what you’ve heard, saying:
translation_text.an4.183:2.6: There’s nothing wrong with talking about what you’ve thought, saying:
translation_text.an4.183:2.8: There’s nothing wrong with talking about what you’ve known, saying:
translation_text.an4.183:3.1: “Brahmin, I don’t say you should talk about everything you see, hear, think, and know.
translation_text.an4.183:3.2: But I also don’t say you should talk about nothing you see, hear, think, and know.
translation_text.an4.183:4.1: When talking about certain things you’ve seen, heard, thought, or known, unskillful qualities grow while skillful qualities decline. I say that you shouldn’t talk about those things.
translation_text.an4.183:4.2: When talking about other things you’ve seen, heard, thought, or known, unskillful qualities decline while skillful qualities grow. I say that you should talk about those things.”
translation_text.an4.183:8.1: Then Vassakāra the brahmin, having approved and agreed with what the Buddha said, got up from his seat and left.
